shell.localeHint: Browser preference is the default until a cookie-based selection is made.

auth.title: Sign in to the ExamOps shell
auth.subtitle: This milestone uses environment-backed bootstrap credentials with signed cookies so the real database-backed auth layer can replace it cleanly later.
auth.error: The email or password was incorrect.
auth.credentialsBody: Set AUTH_BOOTSTRAP_EMAIL, AUTH_BOOTSTRAP_PASSWORD, AUTH_BOOTSTRAP_NAME, AUTH_BOOTSTRAP_ROLE, and AUTH_SECRET in .env before signing in.

dashboard.title: Operations overview
dashboard.subtitle: The app shell, protected routes, and shared UI now sit on top of the bootstrap stack.

shell.title: Shared shell
shell.body: Dashboard routes now share one protected layout, responsive navigation, and common header behavior.

auth.title: Signed session cookies
auth.body: Login and logout are live, and protected routes redirect cleanly when the session is missing.

copy.title: Locale-aware copy
copy.body: Shell copy is centralized so Arabic and English can expand without scattering strings through the tree.

team.title: Team access model
team.subtitle: This screen reflects the current role guard strategy and where shell permissions already apply.

matrix.super_admin: Full platform access, including settings and future user administration.
matrix.coordinator: Operational access with management visibility across shell sections.
matrix.senior: Protected shell access ready for attendance and evaluation slices.

settings.title: Foundation settings preview
settings.subtitle: This route is intentionally restricted to super admins and coordinators to prove the role guard path.

items.auth: Auth is currently environment-backed and signed with AUTH_SECRET.
items.locale: Locale detection uses browser preference first and can be overridden with a cookie.
items.next: Database-backed auth and runtime settings pages are the next expansion point.
